title: News | Sir Mark Hendrick MP
headings: h2: Prizes for 'egg'ceptional Easter Egg competition winners at Deepdale Primary School
h2: Over £900 raised by MP staff team for the Big PNE Sleep-out!
h2: Official Opening of Lumo HQ in Preston
text_excerpt: --> News | Sir Mark Hendrick MP top of page Sir Mark Hendrick Labour and Co-operative MP for Preston Home Contact About News Newsletters Parliament Written Questions Media Gallery More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. mark.hendrick.mp@parliament.uk 01772 883575 All Posts Search Prizes for 'egg'ceptional Easter Egg competition winners at Deepdale Primary School I was delighted to be invited to the Family Easter Egg Assembly at Deepdale Primary School, #Preston to present prizes to Year 5 and 6 winners and their families. Pictured with Headteacher Miss Mirza and Year 6 Senior Teacher, Mrs Woodward. The entries were very creative and imaginative and it’s great to hear that the competition is open to pupils and their families to create a 2D or 3D entry. The entries were ‘egg’ceptional and this annual event has become a much-loved part Mar 27 Over £900 raised by MP staff team for the Big PNE Sleep-out! Well done to everyone who took part in the The Big PNE Sleep Out over the weekend, including members of my staff team who took part for the first time this year. Homelessness is still a concerning issue locally and official statistics from Autumn 2025 indicates that the largest regional increase was in the North West, rising from 367 in 2024 to 441 in 2025 - an increase of 20%. In recent months, I have led campaigns about tackling Violence Against Women and Girls (VAWG), so i Mar 21 Eid Mubarak Wishing all Muslims in #Preston and all my Muslim friends and colleagues, a very Happy Eid. I hope you have a joyous day full of peace and happiness celebrating with loved ones, after a month of fasting, reflection and prayer. #EidulFitr2026 #EidMubarak #Eid2026 Mar 20 Official Opening of Lumo HQ in Preston I’m delighted and honoured to officially open the new Lumo Headquarters in #Preston, ahead of their frequent and low-cost rail services between London and Stirling in Scotland. Pictured with Stuart Jones, Managing Director, First Rail Open Access This is a very exciting development for our City.
